member.name: Luke Murphy
member.synopsis: Luke Murphy is the Labour MP for Basingstoke , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Business
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what progress his Department has made on helping to reduce business energy costs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Every business in the country has paid the price of Britain’s dependence on foreign fossil fuel markets. The government’s clean power mission will end the exposure of industry to the rollercoaster of fossil fuel prices. And we are supporting businesses th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Private Rented Housing: Vulnerable Adults
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what mechanisms his Department uses to monitor (a) the impact of private rented sector reforms on the availability of housing for vulnerable people and (b) local housing authorit
writtenQuestions.answerText: My Department collects data on the private rented sector through the English Housing Survey, which is published annually and can be found on gov.uk here, and through ongoing longitudinal survey of private tenants. My Department also publishes data showing...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Suicide
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what consideration he has given to publishing annual data on suicides among armed forces veterans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Any suicide is a tragedy and is one too many. Our thoughts remain with the families, friends and colleagues of all those affected. In 2024, for the first time ever, the Office for National Statistics (ONS) published annual suicide rates (year 2021) for th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Parents

writtenQuestions.answerText: We’re delivering a modern deal for working parents through the Employment Rights Act. Improving access to flexible working to allow parents to fit work around their family life, and employers will be expected to agree flexible working requests unless ther...

writtenQuestions.answerText: As of 6th February, the Oil and Gas Transition Training Fund has received 980 applications and of these 402 have been approved so far. Following the success of the Oil and Gas Transition Training Fund pilot in Aberdeen City and Aberdeenshire UK and Scotti...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sickle Cell Diseases: Health Services

writtenQuestions.answerText: The NHS England Sickle Cell and Thalassaemia Improvement Programme aims to address health inequalities through targeted interventions. A key priority for the programme has been supporting National Health Service trusts in the management of acute sickle ce...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Further Education: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the inability of Further Education colleges to reclaim VAT on their financial sustainability.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Further Education (FE) funding is vital to ensure people are being trained in the skills they need to thrive in the modern labour market. The 2025 Spending Review provided an additional £1.2 billion per year by 2028-29 for skills and £1.7 billion of capit...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Child Maintenance Service (CMS) formula is calculated on the paying parent’s gross income, the number of qualifying children, overnight care arrangements, and any additional children in their care – known as ‘relevant other children’. The formula does...

writtenQuestions.heading: Arthritis: Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps are being taken to reduce access to treatment variations between ICBs for people with arthritis.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department is committed to improving timely diagnosis and management of arthritis and other musculoskeletal (MSK) conditions across England.The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) has published guidance to support health and care ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving Licences: Foreign Nationals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of extending the 12-month period during which holders of non-designated foreign driving licences are permitted to drive in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This department reviews GB driver licensing arrangements from time to time. Any changes to the current 12-month period during which the holders of non-UK driving licences are permitted to drive in the UK would be subject to appropriate consultation and re...

writtenQuestions.heading: Joint Ventures: Corporation Tax
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether she plans to take steps to stop joint ventures using corporation tax reliefs through the purchase or transfer of trading losses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Group relief allows the transfer of allowable losses from one company to another in the same group. Consortium relief is a type of group relief which allows companies that jointly own another company (a consortium company) to obtain relief for their share...

writtenQuestions.heading: Rheumatology: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps are his Department taking to ensure NHS staff can recognise rheumatological symptoms and ensure urgent referral to rheumatology services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Royal College of General Practitioners has produced e-learning modules on a number of musculoskeletal (MSK) and rheumatic conditions, such as inflammatory arthritis, osteoarthritis, and axial spondyloarthritis, which are designed to help general pract...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not provide specific guidance on the use of second-hand infant car seats and has not evaluated the feasibility of an inspection or certification process for such products. The Royal Society for the Prevention of Accidents (RoSPA) advis...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The maximum duration of two years between passing the theory test and a subsequent practical test is in place to ensure a customer’s road safety knowledge and ability to identify developing hazards is current. This validity period is set in legislation, a...
